pull_request.title: [bugfix] Rewrite transfer metrics from C++ to Python side
pull_request.body:
Previously, transfer metrics (ops_total, bytes_total) were recorded in the C++ layer (transfer.cu, transfer_ssd.cpp, gds_manager.cpp) via MetricsManager macros. Although C++ has its own Prometheus exporter (port 8081), the actual data transfer code runs in forked worker subprocesses, which do not initialize the C++ Prometheus metrics server. As a result, these transfer metrics were never collected at all.

Key changes:
- Rewrite transfer metrics collection entirely on the Python side with a post-completion approach, recording metrics in the main process after transfer operations complete
- Add `flexkv_py_transfer_bytes_total` counter to Python collector for tracking data transfer throughput
- Remove `flexkv_cpp_transfer_ops_total` and `flexkv_cpp_transfer_bytes_total` counters from C++ MetricsManager

Changes include:
- Extended `CompletedOp` in `flexkv/common/transfer.py` with `transfer_type`, `num_blocks`, and `num_bytes` fields
- Compute transfer bytes in `TransferEngine` (`transfer_engine.py`) when an op finishes
- Record all three counters (ops_total, blocks_total, bytes_total) via `record_transfer_completed()` in `KVTaskManager._update_tasks()` (`kvtask.py`), ensuring metrics are collected in the main process
- Added `flexkv_py_transfer_bytes_total` counter to `flexkv/metrics/collector.py`
- Removed old `_record_transfer_ops()` from `cache_engine.py`
- Removed C++ transfer metric recording from `transfer.cu`, `transfer_ssd.cpp`, and `gds_manager.cpp`
- Updated Grafana dashboard (`flexkv-demo.json`): added Transfer Throughput panel, replaced C++ Transfer Throughput with Cache Blocks Rate panel
- Updated monitoring documentation at `docs/monitoring/README_en.md` (English) and `docs/monitoring/README_zh.md` (Chinese)

+                else:

+                    operation,

+            num_bytes: Number of bytes transferred
             operation: Operation type ("get" or "put")
         """
         if not self.enabled:
             return
         self.transfer_ops_total.labels(transfer_type=transfer_type, operation=operation).inc()
         if num_blocks > 0:
             self.transfer_blocks_total.labels(transfer_type=transfer_type, operation=operation).inc(num_blocks)
+        if num_bytes > 0:

commit_summaries.message:
[bugfix] Rewrite transfer metrics from C++ to Python side

Previously, transfer metrics (ops_total, bytes_total) were recorded in
the C++ layer (transfer.cu, transfer_ssd.cpp, gds_manager.cpp) via
MetricsManager macros. Although C++ has its own Prometheus exporter
(port 8081), the actual data transfer code runs in forked worker
subprocesses, which do not initialize the C++ Prometheus metrics server.
As a result, these transfer metrics were never collected at all.

Key changes:
- Rewrite transfer metrics collection entirely on the Python side with
  a post-completion approach, recording metrics in the main process
  after transfer operations complete
- Add `flexkv_py_transfer_bytes_total` counter to Python collector for
  tracking data transfer throughput
- Remove `flexkv_cpp_transfer_ops_total` and
  `flexkv_cpp_transfer_bytes_total` counters from C++ MetricsManager

Changes include:
- Extended `CompletedOp` in `flexkv/common/transfer.py` with
  `transfer_type`, `num_blocks`, and `num_bytes` fields
- Compute transfer bytes in `TransferEngine` (`transfer_engine.py`)
  when an op finishes
- Record all three counters (ops_total, blocks_total, bytes_total) via
  `record_transfer_completed()` in `KVTaskManager._update_tasks()`
  (`kvtask.py`), ensuring metrics are collected in the main process
- Added `flexkv_py_transfer_bytes_total` counter to
  `flexkv/metrics/collector.py`
- Removed old `_record_transfer_ops()` from `cache_engine.py`
- Removed C++ transfer metric recording from `transfer.cu`,
  `transfer_ssd.cpp`, and `gds_manager.cpp`
- Updated Grafana dashboard (`flexkv-demo.json`): added Transfer
  Throughput panel, replaced C++ Transfer Throughput with Cache Blocks
  Rate panel
- Updated monitoring documentation at `docs/monitoring/README_en.md`
  (English) and `docs/monitoring/README_zh.md` (Chinese)
fix: properly register prometheus-cpp submodule in git tree
